title: Diagnose and reduce cold-start latency
description: Separate init from execution duration in traces, find the dominant init cost, and reduce it before reaching for provisioned concurrency.
body: Steps: 1. Pull traces/metrics and split init duration from invocation duration at p50/p99. 2. Confirm spikes correlate with new-instance (cold) starts. 3. Attribute init cost: package size/download, runtime startup, dependency loading, connection setup. 4. Reduce the dominant factor (trim deps, lazy-load, move client init to module scope, smaller runtime). 5. Re-measure; only if still insufficient and latency-critical, apply provisioned concurrency. Rollback point: before enabling provisioned concurrency (a cost change). Validation: measured init p99 drops and tail latency improves; enabling always-on concurrency without first reducing init cost masks the problem and adds spend.
